mood: bright collected-web bloom under a torn macro lens, with a black peripheral hush from the reference image used as breathing space rather than gloom
lighting: clean flash-macro light over warm white and peony color, with shallow shadows under folds, tiny blown highlights on glass seams, and deep quiet edge voids kept away from the main bloom
object_inventory: one oversized cropped bouquet rupture carrying the main source-bearing surface
a diagonal torn bookmark ribbon crossing the bloom like an inserted feed strip
small perforated stamp margins and pixel-grain notches along petal edges
narrow receipt slits and luminous instrument-grid wells embedded in folds
quiet black outer negative space framing the bright central specimen
negative_constraints: no legible words, labels, filenames, logos, handles, or alphanumeric callouts inside the image
no dashboard, desk, office, gallery wall, cabinet, shelf, archive grid, or generic software interface
no summary cards or equal-weight rectangular source tiles
do not copy the visual reference subject or page chrome; borrow only its stark edge silence and compressed contrast
avoid recent black-tape scan field, sparse cloth taxonomy, chrome observatory, nocturne current, porcelain basin, and isometric warm-window section grammar
no equal-weight grid of source objects
no literal depiction of the source reference image
